preview_examples.query: What was said on speech overlap?
references: Efforts by speaker mn005 are in progress to detect overlapping speech. For a single transcribed meeting, speaker mn005 reported approximately 300 cases of overlap. Future work will involve manually deriving time marks from sections of overlapping speech for the same meeting, and then experimenting with different measures, e.g. energy increase, to determine a set of acoustically salient features for identifying speaker overlap.
scoring_references: Efforts by speaker mn005 are in progress to detect overlapping speech. For a single transcribed meeting, speaker mn005 reported approximately 300 cases of overlap. Future work will involve manually deriving time marks from sections of overlapping speech for the same meeting, and then experimenting with different measures, e.g. energy increase, to determine a set of acoustically salient features for identifying speaker overlap.

vanilla_rag.prediction: The discussion centered around the challenge of manually marking speech overlaps in recordings. PhD D mentioned finding three hundred overlapping speech events in a session lasting forty-five minutes, which was described as very time-consuming. PhD D defined an "overlapping zone" as a period where three or more speakers are talking together, and an "acoustic event" as the overlapping zone or any period where more than one voice is produced at the same time. The group considered whether it would be more efficient to mark only the overlaps rather than all acoustic events, as marking all events was found to be very time-consuming. Postdoc B suggested that marking only the overlaps would be more efficient, especially since the participants were not used to dealing with multi-channel transcriptions. The group also discussed the need for an extra pass through the recordings if they wanted to add more refined coding of overlaps.
